question_en: How much water does coffee need?
answer_en: Coffee plants need 1500-2500mm of rainfall per year. In dry periods, irrigate with 40-60mm per week.
answer_ki: Kahũa nĩkarĩa maaĩ mangĩ maingĩ, maingĩ ma 1500-2500mm kaaka. Nĩ rĩrĩa mĩgũnĩ, irigate na 40-60mm per week.

question_en: What fertilizer is best for coffee?
answer_en: Use NPK 20:10:10 or 17:17:17 at planting. Apply CAN during fruiting. Organic compost is also recommended.
answer_ki: Tũmĩra NPK 20:10:10 kana 17:17:17 kũrĩa ya kupanda. Tũmĩra CAN rĩrĩa maĩhĩa macio. Compost nayo nĩ mũrĩo.

question_en: When should I prune coffee trees?
answer_en: Prune coffee after harvesting. Remove dead branches, maintain 2-3 main stems, and shape the canopy.
answer_ki: Pangũra kahũa rĩrĩa kuvuna. Ikĩra maguta marĩtũka, riĩ na mĩtĩ ĩrĩa 2-3, na kũra formatĩ ya canopy.

question_en: What are common coffee pests?
answer_en: Common coffee pests: Coffee Berry Borer, Leaf Miner, Ants, Mealybugs, and Scale insects.
answer_ki: Mahatĩ a kahũa: Coffee Berry Borer, Leaf Miner, Ants, Mealybugs, na Scale insects.

question_en: What diseases affect coffee?
answer_en: Main coffee diseases: Coffee Leaf Rust, Wilt Disease, Berry Rot, and Root Rot. Use resistant varieties.
answer_ki: Mĩrĩa mĩũru ya kahũa: Coffee Leaf Rust, Wilt Disease, Berry Rot, na Root Rot. Tũmĩra varieties ĩrĩa resistant.

question_en: What is the best altitude for coffee?
question_ki: Nĩ atĩa altitude mũgĩrĩ ya kahua?
answer_en: Coffee grows best at 1500-2000m altitude with temperatures between 15-24°C.
answer_ki: Kahũa nĩ gĩkũrũka wega mbuga ya 1500-2000m, na temperature 15-24°C.

question_en: What soil type is best for coffee?
question_ki: Gĩthaka kĩrĩa kĩega kĩa kahua ni kĩ?
answer_en: Coffee prefers well-drained loamy soil with pH 5.5-6.5. Add organic matter for sandy soils.
answer_ki: Kahũa nĩ yĩĩtũka irio ĩrĩa drain wega, loam na pH 5.5-6.5. Ongeza organic matter irio ĩrĩa sandy.

question_en: How do I space coffee seedlings?
question_ki: Nĩ atĩa spacing ya seedlings ya kahua?
answer_en: Space coffee plants 2m x 2m or 2.5m x 2.5m. This gives 1600-2500 plants per hectare.
answer_ki: Pangĩra kahũa mita 2x2 kana 2.5x2.5. Ĩrĩo nĩ 1600-2500 plants per hectare.

question_en: When is coffee harvested?
answer_en: Coffee is harvested 7-9 months after flowering. Pick ripe red cherries selectively.
answer_ki: Kahũa nĩ korwa mĩyaka ĩrĩa 7-9 mũgĩrĩ wa bloom. Kũra cherries ĩrĩa red na selective.

question_en: How do I process coffee cherries?
answer_en: Processing methods: Wet (wash), Dry (natural), or Honey process. Each affects bean flavor.
answer_ki: Mĩthĩka ya processing: Wet (wash), Dry (natural), kana Honey process. Ĩmwe nĩ ĩgĩa flavor ya bean.

question_en: How should coffee be dried?
answer_en: Dry coffee beans on raised beds or mats. Target 10-12% moisture content. Avoid direct sunlight.
answer_ki: Omagĩra beans za kahũa mĩgunda mĩrĩ. Goal nĩ 10-12% moisture. Gũtiga sun direct.

question_en: What is coffee market price?
question_ki: Bei ya coffee soko ni iyi?
answer_en: Coffee prices vary. Quality arabica fetches $1.50-$4.00 per kg at Nairobi Coffee Exchange.
answer_ki: Bei ya kahũa nĩ vary. Quality arabica nĩ $1.50-$4.00 per kg Nairobi Coffee Exchange.

question_en: Can I grow coffee organically?
answer_en: Yes! Use compost, green manure, biological pest control. Organic coffee commands premium prices.
answer_ki: Eya! Tũmĩra compost, green manure, pest control ĩrĩa biological. Organic kahũa nĩ yĩĩtũka premium prices.

question_en: What shade trees for coffee?
question_ki: Mĩtĩ ya shade ya kahua ni iyi?
answer_en: Use shade trees: Grevillea, Albizia, Calliandra, or Leucaena. Provide 30-50% shade.
answer_ki: Tũmĩra shade trees: Grevillea, Albizia, Calliandra, kana Leucaena. Pea 30-50% shade.

question_en: Can I intercrop coffee?
answer_en: Yes! Intercrop with bananas, beans, or maize. Avoid crops that compete for nutrients.
answer_ki: Eya! Intercrop na bananas, beans, kana maize. Gũtiga crops ĩrĩa ĩcompete nutrients.

question_en: How to start coffee nursery?
question_ki: Nĩ atĩa kũra nursery ya kahua?
answer_en: Start with quality seeds or clones. Use polythene bags, well-drained soil. Nursery takes 6-9 months.
answer_ki: Raĩra na quality seeds kana clones. Tũmĩra polythene bags, soil ĩrĩa drain wega. Nursery nĩ 6-9 months.

question_en: When to apply fertilizer to coffee?
question_ki: Nĩ rĩrĩa gĩtĩra mbolea ya kahua?
answer_en: Apply DAP at planting. Top-dress with CAN at flowering and berry development. Split doses are best.
answer_ki: Tũmĩra DAP kũrĩa ya kupanda. Top-dress na CAN rĩrĩa flowering na berry development. Split doses nĩ zĩega.

question_en: How to control coffee pests naturally?
question_ki: Nĩ atĩa kũcontrola mahatĩ ya kahua naturally?
answer_en: Use neem oil, insecticidal soaps, beneficial insects. Keep plantation clean. Prune regularly.
answer_ki: Tũmĩra neem oil, insecticidal soaps, beneficial insects. Keep plantation clean. Pangũra regularly.

question_en: What is coffee cherry disease?
answer_en: Coffee Cherry Disease (CCD) causes berries to rot. Control by pruning, fungicides, and good sanitation.
answer_ki: Coffee Cherry Disease (CCD) nĩ ĩgĩa berries rot. Control na pruning, fungicides, na sanitation ĩega.

question_en: How long do coffee trees live?
question_ki: Kahua mĩtĩ nĩ gĩkũra mĩrĩa mĩrĩ?
answer_en: Coffee trees can produce for 30-50 years with good care. Peak production is years 5-15.
answer_ki: Mĩtĩ ya kahũa nĩ yĩĩtũka years 30-50 na care ĩega. Peak production nĩ years 5-15.
